For this example, we're basically testing if eating fruit every day actually helps lower obesity rates. The null hypothesis is that fruit doesn't do anything for obesity, and the alternative is that fruit does help. A Type I error would be us saying  yeah, fruit lowers obesity  when it actually doesn't, which would have everyone out here thinking fruit is doing more than it really is. A Type II error would be saying fruit has no effect when it actually does, which means we'd miss out on something that could genuinely help people be healthier. Either way, you end up getting the wrong message about whether eating fruit actually matters.
